channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Voters in 13 states have less than a week to register. When is your deadline?
item.description: The 2024 presidential election is fast approaching, and in some states, the voter registration deadline is as soon as this week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal agencies warn of scams in wake of Helene
item.description: “These criminals take advantage of victims before, during, and after a natural disaster strikes, targeting people when they are most vulnerable, and their methods to commit fraud are wide-ranging,” one U.S. Attorney said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What's next after prosecutors reveal new evidence in Trump's 2020 election interference case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Special counsel Jack Smith has provided a road map for how prosecutors hope to prove their case charging former President Donald Trump with an illegal scheme to overturn his 2020 election loss — if it ever gets to trial.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Israel extends evacuation warnings in Lebanon, signaling a wider offensive
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Israeli military on Thursday warned people to evacuate a city and other communities in southern Lebanon that are north of a U.N.-declared buffer zone, signaling that it may widen a ground operation launched earlier this week against the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: FADI TAWIL and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pilot flying Helene rescue missions ordered out, threatened with arrest
item.description: NORTH CAROLINA - When Jordan Seidhom woke up Saturday morning, he saw a Facebook post that tens of thousands of people were commenting on and sharing. A family was stranded on a mountain in Banner Elk, North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Jody Barr, KTLA 5

item.title: Las Vegas casino implosion show will include 150 'Pyrodrones' 400 feet above the Tropicana
item.description: Las Vegas tourists should not run in fear as 150 “Pyrdrones” shoot sparks 400 feet above the Tropicana demolition site next week—because it’s all part of the show.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bipartisan California lawmakers urge House leaders to boost disaster relief funding
item.description: A bipartisan group of lawmakers from Orange County, Calif., are urging House leaders to include disaster relief funding in fiscal 2025 spending, highlighting wildfire damage in the state even as damage from Hurricane Helene on the other side of the country has heightened attention on the issue.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Florida law banning homeless people from sleeping in public spaces takes effect
item.description: Under the law, municipalities are required to create designated spaces for camping and sleeping if homeless shelters are full.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Grandparents found hugging after being killed during Hurricane Helene
item.description: South Carolina residents Marcia and Jerry Savage were found dead, lying in bed and hugging each other, after a tree fell on their bedroom during Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Black leaders in New York are grappling with Eric Adams and representation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It wasn’t a shock to many Black New Yorkers that Mayor Eric Adams has surrounded himself with African American civil rights leaders, clergy and grassroots activists since his indictment last week on federal bribery charges.
item.dc:creator: AARON MORRISON and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Voters in 13 states have less than a week to register. When is the deadline in Illinois?
item.description: The 2024 presidential election is fast approaching, and in some states, the voter registration deadline is as soon as this week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, WGN 9

item.title: Wawa named No. 1 convenience store in consumer satisfaction survey
item.description: Analysts noted that many of the top-ranked chains specialized in ready-to-eat foods prepared in the stores' kitchens.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, KRON4

item.title: Irate residents halt construction of migrant shelter
item.description: Residents of a southeast Juarez neighborhood are demanding the government call off plans to build a migrant shelter near their children’s schools.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Giuliani's daughter: ‘The biggest threat to our country is Trump’
item.description: Former New York City Mayor Rudy Giuliani's (R) daughter said she thinks the biggest threat to America is former President Trump. Caroline Giuliani has spoken out in recent days about her father’s entanglements with Trump and the upcoming election.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: Helene waves and storm surge crash into Florida home as owners watch
item.description: Terrifying moments caught on video showed Hurricane Helene’s powerful and deadly storm surge as it crashed into a home in Pinellas County last week.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, KRON4

item.title: Florida communities hit three times by hurricanes grapple with how and whether to rebuild
item.description: HORSESHOE BEACH, Fla. (AP) — It was just a month ago that Brooke Hiers left the state-issued emergency trailer where her family had lived since Hurricane Idalia slammed into her Gulf Coast fishing village of Horseshoe Beach in August 2023.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and DAVID R. MARTIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Explainer: How immigrants end up on ICE's non-detained docket
item.description: Recent reports that over 435,000 convicted criminals are on U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement's non-detained docket (NDD) -- meaning they are not detained by the agency while they await immigration proceedings -- has spurred much criticism and confusion.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: South Carolina grandparents killed by fallen tree during Helene are found hugging
item.description: A South Carolina couple were found hugging each other after a tree fell on their bedroom and killed them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Liz Cheney to join Harris on campaign trail at birthplace of GOP
item.description: Cheney and her father, former GOP vice president Dick Cheney, endorsed Harris last month over GOP nominee Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Costco now selling platinum bars and coins on website
item.description: Following the smash success of adding gold and silver bars and coins to its list of products, Costco has added a new precious metal to its stores: platinum.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Liz Cheney will campaign with Harris in Wisconsin while Trump holds a rally in Michigan
item.description: Liz Cheney is one of Donald Trump's fiercest Republican critics and now she's campaigning with Democrat Kamala Harris in Wisconsin.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The flood of ghost guns is slowing after regulation. It's also being challenged in the Supreme Court
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Guy Boyd was hanging out with friends he had known for years in Ann Arbor, Michigan, on the night an accidental gunshot tore into his head.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge's death caught on ‘incredibly damning’ video: Attorney
item.description: Prosecutors played the short, silent video during a preliminary hearing for former Letcher County Sheriff Shawn "Mickey" Stines.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Mormon faith pushes ahead with global temple building boom despite cool reception in Las Vegas
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A historic building boom of big, bright Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints temples — beacons to the faithful with steeples pointing heavenward around the world — is meeting resistance in some parts of the U.S., including one place not really known for moderation.
item.dc:creator: KEN RITTER and HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane Kirk strengthens into a Category 3 storm in the Atlantic
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Hurricane Kirk strengthened Wednesday into a Category 3 storm in the Atlantic Ocean and was expected to grow rapidly into a major hurricane, forecasters said. There were no coastal watches or warnings in effect, and the storm system was not yet deemed a threat to land.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fed agencies released noncitizens without ID into US, allowed them to board domestic flights: DHS OIG report
item.description: A new DHS Inspector General report finds CBP, ICE, and TSA allowed noncitizens without ID to enter the U.S. and board domestic flights.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nearly a third of car owners 'underwater': Survey
item.description: More car owners are finding themselves owing more on their loans than their cars are worth, says the survey.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Shock of deadly floods is a reminder of Appalachia’s risk from violent storms in a warming climate
item.description: HENDERSONVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Hurricane Helene dumped trillions of gallons of water hundreds of miles inland, devastating communities nestled in mountains far from the threat of storm surge or sea level rise.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oakland police union demands transparency on sale of former A's stadium amid concerns of public safety cuts
item.description: Oakland officials are floating questions about the sale of the city's share of its stake in the Oakland Coliseum, former home of the A's baseball team.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US school-entry vaccination rates fall as exemptions keep rising
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. kindergarten vaccination rates dipped last year and the proportion of children with exemptions rose to an all-time high, according to federal data posted Wednesday. The share of kids exempted from vaccine requirements rose to 3.3%, up from 3% the year before.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dozens linked to Los Angeles-area White supremacist gang nabbed in federal drug, weapons trafficking probe
item.description: Suspected members of the San Fernando Valley Peckerwoods, a L.A.-area White supremacist gang, were arrested and indicted Wednesday on drug, weapons trafficking and fraud charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Northern border sector sees over 19K illegal immigrants caught in FY 24, more than past 17 years combined
item.description: The 19,222 illegal immigrants caught in fiscal year 2024 was a higher figure than the past 17 years combined, authorities said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CNN launches digital paywall for the first time
item.description: CNN, one of the most visited news sites in the world, announced Tuesday that visitors will soon have to pay to view the website. CNN.com will implement a paywall, asking visitors to pay $3.99 a month or a discounted rate of $29.99 a year to access its news content.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Shoppers are panic-buying toilet paper amid port strike, but experts say there’s no need
item.description: “Hope you have some toilet paper stock piled. It’s started. Sam’s and Costco are depleted,” one user said on the social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: Online voting in Alaska’s Fat Bear Week contest starts after an attack killed 1 contestant
item.description: Viewers will soon pick their favorite among a dozen brown bears in the annual Fat Bear Week contest at Katmai National Park and Preserve in Alaska.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Submerged EVs can catch fire when inundated with saltwater: What to know
item.description: Electric vehicles can catch fire if they are inundated by saltwater, so owners who live in the path of a major storm should take precautions and prepare for the possibility that they'll be unable to charge their cars during a power outage.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Fox 5

item.title: Florida Republican 'absolutely certain' there will be supplemental to address Helene aid
item.description: Rep. Kat Cammack (R-Fla.) said Monday she’s “absolutely certain” Congress will consider a supplemental aid package to provide additional disaster relief to areas devastated by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5
